div1.entryFree: Harrow (cf. Harry vb.), to vex, to fill with distress and anguish (Anglosaxon herevjan): let the Volsces plough Rome and h. Italy, Cor. V, 3, 34 (quibbling). it --s me with fear and wonder, Hml. I, 1, 44 (Qq horrors and horrows): a tale whose lightest word would h. up thy soul, I, 5, 16.
